business.headline: Swiggy Instamart receives 9 notices from food regulator over quality complaintsFSSAI has issued nine notices to Swiggy Instamart over consumer complaints about expired, spoiled and contaminated food deliveries. The regulator has sought detailed compliance records and warned of legal action if the response is delayed.

Apple sues OpenAI, ex-employees over alleged trade secrets theftApple has sued OpenAI and two former employees, alleging misuse of trade secrets for consumer hardware. The case sharpens tensions between partners as both companies push deeper into AI devices.

RBI auctions Rs 32,000 crore in govt bonds: Are they among the safest investments?Government bonds are back in the spotlight after the RBI's latest Rs 32,000 crore auction. What makes them different from other investments, and who are they best suited for? Have a look.

8th Pay Commission: Why its impact goes far beyond government salariesEconomists say the 8th Pay Commission will affect far more than central government salaries and pensions. Its recommendations could shape consumer demand, state finances, inflation and fiscal planning.

File Early, Stay Relaxed: Why the Income Tax Department is sending SMS alertsThousands of taxpayers are receiving SMS reminders from the Income Tax Department asking them to file their ITR early. What's behind these messages?

Sensex ends 828 points higher, Nifty above 24,200; RIL up 2%The BSE Sensex surged 827.57 points, or 1.08%, to close at 77,569.39, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 244.10 points, or 1.02%, to settle at 24,206.90.

Why wait for the IPO? Some investors are buying shares much earlier. You can tooLong before an IPO opens, and sometimes even years before a company files its draft papers with the market regulator, its shares can already be bought and sold legally in what's known as the unlisted or pre-IPO market.

Income tax department launches ITR-7 Excel utility for AY 2026-27With the utility now live, eligible taxpayers can download it, fill in the required information offline, validate the details, generate a JSON file and upload it on the Income Tax e-Filing portal to complete the return filing process.

TCS's hiring surprise: Why India's biggest IT firm is adding jobs againTCS added 9,279 employees in the April-June quarter after steep headcount declines in late 2025. The shift reflects selective hiring, redeployment and rising demand for AI and digital skills.

Govt eyes stronger forex reserves; FM likely to meet banks soonThe government's move comes at a time when India's foreign exchange reserves have declined sharply. The reserves fell from around $728 billion in February to nearly $682 billion by the end of May.

Home loan on a rented house? Here's what you can still claim under new tax regimeAs taxpayers file their ITRs, understanding home loan tax rules has become more important than ever. In this edition of 'KNOW YOUR TAX', we explain what you can still claim on a rented property under the new tax regime.

TCS, Infosys, HCLTech rise up to 4%: Why are IT stocks rising today?TCS's stronger-than-expected June-quarter results pushed IT stocks higher on Friday. The Nifty IT index climbed 1.82%, while the BSE Focused IT index gained nearly 2%.

Sensex jumps 800 points: Why is stock market rising today?Around 10 am, the BSE Sensex was trading 800.61 points, or 1.04%, higher at 77,542.43, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 247.80 points, or 1.03%, to 24,210.60.

Sensex, Nifty open in green lifted by IT stocks as TCS earnings cheer marketsThe BSE Sensex opened at 77,395.63 and climbed 676.12 points, or 0.88%, to 77,417.94 in early trade. The NSE Nifty50 rose 196.30 points, or 0.82%, to 24,159.10. The benchmarks had also ended higher in the previous session after recovering from Wednesday's sharp sell-off.

Why TCS may have to rethink its dividend to win the AI raceTCS is winning more AI work, but analysts say scaling it will need heavier investment. That could push the company to rebalance dividends and future growth spending.

EPF Scheme 2026: What it is, who benefits and what it means for youFrom simpler withdrawal rules to new safeguards for retirement savings, the EPF Scheme, 2026 brings several important changes. Here's a quick look at who stands to benefit and what the new rules mean for you.

Tax regime to e-verify: Income Tax Department's 7 golden rules for ITR filingThe Income Tax Department has issued seven golden rules for filing ITRs for AY 2026-27. The checklist urges early preparation, careful verification and timely e-verification to avoid delays and lost benefits.

TCS adds over 9,200 employees in Q1, logs biggest hiring in over a yearAlongside higher hiring, TCS reported a solid financial performance for the June quarter. Consolidated net profit stood at Rs 13,349 crore, while revenue rose 14% year-on-year to Rs 72,275 crore.

TCS Q1 results: Revenue jumps 14%; IT major declares Rs 12 interim dividendThe company posted double-digit growth in both revenue and profit, helped by steady demand from clients, especially in the banking and financial services sector. It also announced an interim dividend of Rs 12 per share for its shareholders.

Sensex, Nifty close higher despite US-Iran tensions; TCS results in focusBenchmark indices closed higher on Thursday as investors returned to beaten-down stocks after Wednesday's sharp sell-off. The rebound was led by midcaps and realty shares, while Middle East tensions, crude prices and earnings remained in focus.

entertainment.headline: Dhamaal 4 box office Day 2: Comedy crosses Rs 50 crore globally after Saturday jump Ajay Devgn's Dhamaal 4 posed a sharp first-Saturday jump, taking its worldwide gross past Rs 50 crore in two days. The comedy held steady despite multiple releases and strengthened its opening weekend run.

Sohail Khan opens up on divorce from Seema Sajdeh: I lost someone I truly loved Sohail Khan and Seema Sajdeh shared a warm reunion after she entered Alliance as a wildcard contestant. He said work troubles affected his state of mind during their marriage, while the show helped them reconnect.

Priyanka Chopra reunites with Andaaz co-star Lara Dutta at Wimbledon Priyanka Chopra and Lara Dutta reunited at Wimbledon and shared the moment online. Their meeting revived Andaaz memories and sparked warm reactions from fans.

Ssunita Ahuja confirms Bollywood debut with son Yash after Lock Upp exit Ssunita Ahuja says she and her son Yash will debut together in an Ektaa Kapoor-backed film releasing in September. She will play his mother on screen and has asked fans to bless Yash as warmly as they have supported her.

With you I feel complete: Akansha Ranjan tears up reading vows to Sharan Sharma A heartfelt video from Akansha Ranjan and Sharan Sharma's wedding has surfaced online, capturing the actor getting emotional as she read out her vows to her husband during their intimate Mumbai ceremony.

Ram Kapoor shares childhood trauma on Lock Upp: I was molested at 13 Actor Ram Kapoor disclosed on Lock Upp: Sach Ya Sazaa that he was molested at 13 in boarding school. The revelation left the house in tears and prompted him to speak about trauma, apology and healing.

Prithviraj Sukumaran reveals why he avoids risky stunts: I have 3 titanium screws Prithviraj Sukumaran said his 2023 stunt injury changed how he approaches action scenes in I, Nobody. He said the accident, and another injury on set, reminded him of the risks and responsibility involved.

Singer Jasmine Sandlas announces engagement, introduces fiance at Delhi concert Singer Jasmine Sandlas introduced fiance Shekhar Chaudhary on stage and announced their engagement at her Delhi tour opener. Several videos from the special moment have gone viral.

Newlyweds Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce make hearts melt at friend's wedding Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce were photographed at JuJu Smith-Schuster and Laura Kruk's wedding in California, marking their first public appearance together since their marriage.

Irreparable loss: PM Modi, President Murmu pay tribute to playback legend S Janaki Prime Minister Narendra Modi and President Droupadi Murmu paid tribute to playback singer S Janaki after her death in Mysuru at 88. They acknowledged her contribution to music and praised her six-decade legacy.

Lenin box office Day 2: Akhil Akkineni film sees growth, earns Rs 23 crore globally Actor Akhil Akkineni's Lenin recorded a strong jump in collections on its second day in theatres. Rising occupancy and steady overseas numbers underlined solid opening weekend traction.

Won't let any Tom, dick, Harry insult my daughters: Khushbu Sundar warns trolls Actor Khushbu Sundar defended daughters Avantika and Anandita after online remarks about their appearance following Anandita's wedding. The actor warned trolls and wrote how she wouldn't tolerate anyone using their daughters for fame.

The Odyssey Mumbai screening: Christopher Nolan, Tom Holland, Matt Damon grab chai Christopher Nolan, Matt Damon and Tom Holland stopped for chai at a Mumbai cafe before The Odyssey premiere. The brief visit, added a local touch ahead of the red-carpet screening.

Lenin box office day 1: Akhil Akkineni's film delivers his career-best opening Actor Akhil Akkineni's Lenin opened in theatres on July 10 and witnessed a strong first day at the box office. The makers claimed a career-best opening, though trade estimates differ in terms of worldwide total.

Akansha Ranjan, Sharan Sharma marry in intimate ceremony; Alia Bhatt leads wishes Akansha Ranjan married filmmaker Sharan Sharma in an intimate Mumbai ceremony on July 11. The wedding drew warm wishes from film friends, including actors Alia Bhatt and Ananya Panday.

Singer S Janaki dies; CM Vijay, Rajinikanth mourn Nightingale of South India Veteran playback singer S Janaki, known as Nightingale of South India, died at the age of 88 in Mysore. Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay and actors Rajinikanth, Kamal Haasan and Chiranjeevi paid tribute to the late singer.

After ZEE5 takedown, Satluj spreads across Punjab villages via private screenings After the government ordered Satluj off ZEE5 in India, villagers in Punjab began holding private screenings. The film's spread through pen drives and peer networks has turned the takedown into a wider debate on memory, censorship and politics.

Jana Nayagan coming soon, says CM Vijay after film's A rating Actor Vijay has confirmed Jana Nayagan's censor certification and is headed for its theatrical release. The announcement follows a prolonged dispute with the censor board resolved after 12 suggested modifications.

Why Christopher Nolan chose Mumbai? Real reason behind The Odyssey India premiere Director Christopher Nolan has opened up on the real reason behind holding The Odyssey's India premiere in Mumbai, linking the visit to his previous experience of the city's vibrant film culture and audiences.

general news.headline: India condemns attack on ship off Oman carrying 11 Indians; 10 rescued, 1 missingNew Delhi said that while 10 of the Indian nationals have been rescued, one remains missing. It added that the Indian Embassy in Muscat is closely monitoring the situation.

Gulf on edge as Tehran retaliates after US hits 140 Iranian targets in fresh strikes  Live UpdatesMissile and drone threats spread across the Gulf after fresh US strikes on Iran, prompting Kuwait, Bahrain, Qatar and the UAE to activate emergency security measures. Iran claimed attacks on multiple US bases in the Gulf and warned that "the era of one-sided deals is over" as tensions escalated.

After Satluj, another film is in eye of a storm. Industry is seeking curbs on itJust ahead of its July 24 release, The India Story: Slow Poison in Progress, has come under fire over its claims on pesticides, food safety and rising cancer cases in India. The agrochemical industry has rushed to the CBFC, asking it to scrutinise the data points shown in the teaser. A legal notice has been served too.

No change: Conductor asks Karnataka minister to get off bus during surprise checkTravelling undercover, Karnataka Transport Minister Byrathi Suresh was asked to get off a BMTC bus after offering a Rs 100 note without exact change. The episode, followed by an autorickshaw fare dispute, mirrored everyday commuter complaints in Bengaluru.

What happens behind the scenes at military wives' parties?For many civilians, military wives are synonymous with officers' mess parties and Ladies' Nights. Their lives, however, revolve just as much around repeated relocations, rebuilding careers, raising families, welfare work and creating support systems wherever the next posting takes them.

Is cricket's DRS better than football's VAR?Football and cricket embraced video reviews to eliminate costly mistakes. But one sport gave players the power to challenge decisions, while the other left it to officials, shaping every debate around VAR and DRS since.

Forced to drink cow urine: Sena UBT leader's daughter-in-law alleges black magicGirija Raut, the daughter-in-law of former Shiv Sena (UBT) MP Vinayak Raut, has alleged she suffered years of mental and physical harassment by her in-laws, and was forced to drink cow urine and strands of her hair plucked for black magic rituals.

Meet 12-year-old Mana Jampala who built an AI startup with clients in 3 countriesMana Jampala is only 12, but she is already running an AI startup with deployments in Canada, India and Cambodia. After learning Python at nine and building AI products by 11, the Grade 7 student has created tools that help businesses automate customer calls and everyday work.

Sitar and piano can share a stage. But a perfect jugalbandi defies physicsA sitar and a piano can share a stage, but they can never truly agree on a single note. One is tuned to the pure ratios of physics, the other to a grid that deliberately breaks them, and here is the arithmetic behind that centuries-old divide.

India's July sky looks like April: Why monsoon has hit pause buttonSatellite images of India in mid-July 2026 look eerily like April, with clear seas and bare skies over the core monsoon zone. The IMD explains why the rains have paused, which regions will still get drenched, and when the monsoon is likely to revive.

Preach patience, practice panic: How India failed Sooryavanshi and Samson in the UKIndia's 0-6 tour of the United Kingdom will be remembered for the records it shattered, but perhaps its biggest failure lay elsewhere. In the space of one disastrous series, India risked damaging the confidence of two batters at opposite ends of their careers, raising uncomfortable questions about the team's handling of Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and Sanju Samson.

15 Lava Mobiles associates on company trip among Vietnam boat tragedy victimsA Lava Mobiles incentive trip turned fatal when a tourist boat carrying Indian travellers capsized off Phu Quoc Island in Vietnam. The deaths of 15 Indians have triggered repatriation efforts and an investigation into the accident.

Why marriage doesn't have to mean doing everything togetherHandcrafted ByOur Editors A couple that eats together, does everything together, stays together? Maybe. But happily, though? For too long, constant togetherness has been seen as the hallmark of a happy marriage. Maybe it's time to rethink that.

Messi's streak ends, but Argentina scrape into semis to keep World Cup dream aliveFIFA World Cup: Lionel Messi could not deliver another rescue act, but Argentina found a new hero. Julián Álvarez's stunning extra-time strike inspired a 3-1 win over 10-man Switzerland to send the defending champions into the semi-finals and keep their title defence alive.

I was molested at 13: Ram Kapoor shares childhood trauma on Lock UppActor Ram Kapoor disclosed on Lock Upp: Sach Ya Sazaa that he was molested at 13 in boarding school. The revelation left the house in tears and prompted him to speak about trauma, apology and healing.

Delivery man forcefully enters Bengaluru woman's home, flashes at her; arrestedThe accused flashed the woman after delivering an order at her apartment in Munnekolala area in Bengaluru. The incident came to light after she shared a video of the incident online.

Main Vaapas Aaunga changed how I saw my grandmother's dementiaAfter watching Imtiaz Ali's Main Vaapas Aaunga, I found myself questioning whether memories truly disappear, or whether they simply begin speaking a language only the person living with dementia can understand.
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!

How an Indian startup is using software to break China's rare earth monopolyHandcrafted ByOur Editors Meet the Bengaluru startup that wants to end China's hold on magnet-powered motors through deep-tech sovereignty and out of the box thinking.

Messi's heated exchange with Portuguese referee goes viral: Don't be disrespectfulLionel Messi found himself at the center of another viral World Cup moment, this time after a heated exchange with Portuguese referee João Pinheiro during Argentina's dramatic 3-1 extra-time win over Switzerland.

Semifinals time: England vs Argentina, Spain vs France promise epic showdownsEngland will face Argentina and Spain will meet France in the 2026 FIFA World Cup semi-finals. The last-four line-up keeps alive a potential Messi-Mbappe final rematch from Qatar.

India's rain deficit had improved to 14%. How did it worsen in two daysDays after widespread rain revived hopes for a strong monsoon, a fresh shift in weather patterns has raised new questions about the rest of the season.

A freer Zverev, an unstoppable Sinner. Wimbledon final promises blockbusterWimbledon 2026: Fresh off his maiden Grand Slam triumph at Roland Garros, Alexander Zverev faces his toughest test yet as world No.1 Jannik Sinner stands between him and a second straight major title.

Dharmendra Pradhan orders action against NCERT officials in paper procurement caseNCERT's decision to blacklist a paper supplier came under judicial scrutiny after the council failed to appear before the Delhi High Court, prompting Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan to order an inquiry into the matter.

sports.headline: India tour of England, Zimbabwe: Prince and Bishnoi named injury replacements India have brought in Prince Yadav and Ravi Bishnoi after Harshit Rana and Varun Chakaravarthy suffered hamstring injuries during the recently-concluded England T20Is. The reshuffle comes ahead of the ODIs in England and the T20Is in Zimbabwe.

Is cricket's DRS better than football's VAR? Football and cricket embraced video reviews to eliminate costly mistakes. But one sport gave players the power to challenge decisions, while the other left it to officials, shaping every debate around VAR and DRS since.

Lionel Scaloni dismisses Argentina-England history before World Cup semi-final FIFA World Cup: Argentina coach Lionel Scaloni played down his country's history with England, insisting that the semi-final was "just a football game" after his side defeated Switzerland 3-1 in the quarter-finals.

IPL puts heavy make-up on batters: Sanjay Manjrekar on India's T20I debacle Former India batter Sanjay Manjrekar believes India's disappointing T20I tour should shift the focus from the players to the IPL, saying the tournament's batting-friendly conditions do not adequately prepare batters for the demands of overseas cricket.

Preach patience, practice panic: How India failed Sooryavanshi and Samson in the UK India's 0-6 tour of the United Kingdom will be remembered for the records it shattered, but perhaps its biggest failure lay elsewhere. In the space of one disastrous series, India risked damaging the confidence of two batters at opposite ends of their careers, raising uncomfortable questions about the team's handling of Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and Sanju Samson.

Argentina bias at FIFA World Cup? Swiss coach slams unacceptable Embolo red card Switzerland coach Murat Yakin blasted the rule behind Breel Embolo's controversial red card as "unacceptable" after Argentina scored twice in extra time to seal a 3-1 win and end the Swiss fairytale in the FIFA World Cup quarter-finals on Saturday.

Messi's heated exchange with Portuguese referee goes viral: Don't be disrespectful Lionel Messi found himself at the center of another viral World Cup moment, this time after a heated exchange with Portuguese referee João Pinheiro during Argentina's dramatic 3-1 extra-time win over Switzerland.

A freer Zverev, an unstoppable Sinner. Wimbledon final promises blockbuster Wimbledon 2026: Fresh off his maiden Grand Slam triumph at Roland Garros, Alexander Zverev faces his toughest test yet as world No.1 Jannik Sinner stands between him and a second straight major title.

Semifinals time: England vs Argentina, Spain vs France promise epic showdowns England will face Argentina and Spain will meet France in the 2026 FIFA World Cup semi-finals. The last-four line-up keeps alive a potential Messi-Mbappe final rematch from Qatar.

Messi's streak ends, but Argentina scrape into semis to keep World Cup dream alive FIFA World Cup: Lionel Messi could not deliver another rescue act, but Argentina found a new hero. Julián Álvarez's stunning extra-time strike inspired a 3-1 win over 10-man Switzerland to send the defending champions into the semi-finals and keep their title defence alive.

Conor McGregor's comeback ends in injury heartbreak: What happened at UFC 329Conor McGregor's UFC 329 comeback ended 69 seconds in after a leg injury against Max Holloway. The stoppage deepened doubts over his future and handed Holloway a technical knockout win.

Breel Embolo devastated after costly blunder vs Argentina ends his World Cup dreamFIFA World Cup: Breel Embolo was left in tears after the Switzerland striker was sent off for simulation during his side's quarter-final against Argentina on Saturday.

Erling Haaland's father blasts referee after Norway's World Cup exit to EnglandAlf-Inge Haaland, the father of Norway striker Erling Haaland, criticised the referee after England's 2-1 extra-time win over Norway in the FIFA World Cup quarter-final on Saturday.

Whatever: Bellingham annoyed by Thomas Tuchel's criticism after England beat NorwayJude Bellingham appeared annoyed by Thomas Tuchel's criticism of England's performance after scoring twice to guide the Three Lions to a 2-1 extra-time victory over Norway in the FIFA World Cup quarter-finals on Saturday.

Bellingham bails England out as Haaland's quiet night ends Norway's dreamFIFA World Cup 2026: England are through, but they know sterner tests await if they are to lift the World Cup. Norway bow out with heads held high, having announced themselves as a force capable of troubling the very best.

Why England's controversial goal vs Norway was valid: ExplainedFIFA has explained why Jude Bellingham's controversial equaliser against Norway was allowed to stand, saying Connected Ball Technology found no evidence of contact with an overhead cable despite protests from the Norwegian players.

ARG 3-1 SUI highlights: Argentina storm into semis, to face EnglandArgentina vs Switzerland live, FIFA World Cup quarterfinal: (ARG 3-1 SUI) Julian Alvarez's extra-time stunner and Lautaro Martinez's late clincher fired Argentina past a stubborn 10-man Switzerland 3-1 after extra time, setting up a mouth-watering semi-final clash against England.

India Women within touching distance of Lord's Test triumph after commanding Day 2Kranti Gaud claimed her maiden five-wicket haul in red-ball cricket before Smriti Mandhana's unbeaten 69 helped India Women stretch their lead to 269 against England, leaving the visitors in complete control after Day 2 of the Lord's Test.

NOR 1-2 ENG Highlights: Bellingham fires England past Norway into semi-finalsNorway vs England Highlights, FIFA World Cup quarterfinal: (NOR 1-2 ENG) England are through to the World Cup semi-finals after edging Norway 2-1 in a thrilling quarter-final. Jude Bellingham's extra-time winner seals victory as the Three Lions survive a tense late barrage to book their last-four spot.

Shreyas Iyer faces the media, explains Vaibhav Sooryavanshi snub after poor UK tourENG vs IND, 5th T20I: Shreyas Iyer defended India's decision to drop Vaibhav Sooryavanshi for the final T20I against England, saying the team wanted to test a different opening combination. The India captain also reflected on the team's disastrous 4-0 UK tour and his captaincy.

Linda Noskova cries tears of joy, dedicates title to mother who died from cancerLinda Noskova broke down in tears after defeating Karolina Muchova 6-2, 5-7, 6-3 to win her maiden Wimbledon title, dedicating the triumph to her mother, Ivana, who died after a battle with cancer in 2024.

India lose No.1 T20I ranking after UK tour ends with a stinker in SouthamptonIndia return from the tour of UK without winning a single match. First a 0-2 series loss against Ireland, and then an abject surrender of 0-4 against England, this is one of the lowest moments of the Indian cricket team in the past few years.

Linda Noskova outlasts Karolina Muchova to clinch maiden Wimbledon titleLinda Noskova defeated Karolina Muchova 6-2, 5-7, 6-3 in a thrilling all-Czech final to win her maiden Grand Slam title and become the youngest Wimbledon champion since Petra Kvitova in 2011.

Sacked once before, India fielding coach unlikely to get another extensionIndia fielding coach T Dilip is unlikely to receive a contract extension after the ongoing white-ball tour of England, India Today has learnt. While the BCCI is expected to retain Morne Morkel and Ryan ten Doeschate, Dilip's second stint with the national team appears to be nearing its end.

Record partnership, highest total: Buttler, Brook add insult to India's injuryJos Buttler and Harry Brook smashed a record 233-run opening partnership as England posted 257/3 against India in the fifth T20I at Southampton. The total is England's highest-ever against India in T20Is and leaves the visitors staring at the possibility of losing their No. 1 ICC ranking.

South Africa footballer, 25, dies days after featuring in FIFA World CupSouth Africa international Jayden Adams has died at the age of 25, just weeks after representing his country at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Adams featured in all three group-stage matches as South Africa reached the knockout rounds for the first time in their history.

Rusty Argentina seek inspiration beyond Lionel Messi in quarter-final clashFIFA World Cup, Argentina vs Switzerland preview: Lionel Messi was left in tears after Argentina just about scrapped through to the quarter-final of their tournament. The rusty defending champions will have to pull out all their tricks to beat a disciplined Switzerland side on Sunday, July 12.

Kranti Gaud rocks England at Lord's, becomes first woman on iconic Honours BoardKranti Gaud etched her name into Lord's history on Saturday, becoming the first woman to feature on the iconic Test Honours Board after claiming a maiden five-wicket haul in Test cricket.

Sunil Gavaskar blasts India after team drops Vaibhav Sooryavanshi from 5th T20ILegendary Sunil Gavaskar blasted the Indian team management after the visiting team dropped Vaibhav Sooryavanshi from their playing XI in their final T20I of the England series. Gavaskar said that he was very disappointed with coaching staff's call.

Final Highlights: Linda Noskova beats Karolina Muchova, wins maiden SlamWimbledon Final Live Updates: Linda Noskova has won her maiden Grand Slam title after beating Karolina Muchova 6-2, 5-7, 6-3 in the women's singles final. Loskova dropped five championship points in the second set, but managed to finish it off clinically.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
